evals.prompt: How should I design save-game serialization and level loading? I was going to just use JSON. Also, should I build an event system / Observer to decouple my game logic?
evals.expected_output: Explains parsing as read+validate (validate fully at load, no downstream uncertainty) with custom-binary-vs-JSON tradeoffs, gives concrete level-loading guidance (packed binary, load whole file then parse, parallelize, profile first), and offers an event-system counterpoint (queues often overrated; prefer sequential code, a thread, or deliberate polling) — framing the skepticism as Jonathan Blow's stance while keeping the durable kernels.
expectations: Explains that parsing is read+validate and to validate fully at load so there's no downstream uncertainty; gives custom-binary-vs-JSON tradeoffs
Gives concrete asset/level-loading guidance (e.g. ship a packed binary, load whole file then parse, parallelize, profile before optimizing)
Provides an event-system counterpoint: an event queue is often overrated; prefer sequential code / a thread / deliberate polling; reacting at an arbitrary time risks crashes
Frames JSON/event-system skepticism as Jonathan Blow's stance while keeping the durable kernels
